scope.summary: Cluster B (CI velocity tranche 1) is decomposed into six tracked children rather than landed whole. Its server-side scope is verified complete in the tree; its driver-side scope is carried forward explicitly so it is not silently dropped when the cluster closes.
out_of_scope: The driver-side tranche (plan §25.2 P0.1-P0.4 in rust-oracledb) — carried forward as oraclemcp-dapll, NOT done.
Any code change: every tranche-1 item was already satisfied in this repo.

entry_points.name: Cluster B tranche-1 decomposition into tracked children
entry_points.trace: Six children filed with --deps discovered-from on this cluster: oraclemcp-ysu24 (_quality.yml relocation, verified), oraclemcp-ircfv (powerset disk preflight, verified), oraclemcp-r6agn (release.yml job-graph parallelization, verified), oraclemcp-yi5bu (pre-tag release rehearsal, verified), oraclemcp-m4kua (tag-gating guard test, open), oraclemcp-dapll (driver-side tranche, open). Each verified child carries its own evidence document naming the files and commands checked.

limitations: This close reports that the cluster is DECOMPOSED and that its server-side items were already satisfied — not that Cluster B's full 'both repos' scope is delivered. oraclemcp-dapll remains open and untouched.
Two children are open by design: oraclemcp-m4kua (a guard test I wrote, mutation-tested, and deleted because it did not fail when I removed a tag guard) and oraclemcp-dapll (driver repo).
Verification of pre-existing state, not new implementation. Static and local only; no CI run was dispatched and the full workspace gate was not run for this close.

The children could not be closed before this cluster: scripts/bead_tracker_guard.sh treats an open 'discovered-from' parent as a blocking dependency, so the parent had to close first. That ordering is a tooling constraint, not a statement that the cluster finished before its children — see oraclemcp-r3sti.
follow_ups: oraclemcp-dapll: the driver-side CI velocity tranche is unstarted and belongs to whoever owns rust-oracledb.
oraclemcp-m4kua: the release.yml tag-gating guard test still needs a version that demonstrably fails under mutation.
oraclemcp-r3sti: bead_tracker_guard treats discovered-from as blocking, which forces parents to close before their follow-ups.
